description: A filesystem path / directory entry — the addressable unit of repo-on-disk navigation. One FsPath instance is written into each directory as `path.json`. Tag fields (`parent`, `children`, `siblings`, `breadcrumb`, etc.) are auto-computed by `guild/web/scripts/paths/gen-fs-paths.py` so the repo becomes self-describing: every dir knows where it sits, what it contains, and which peers it maps to.

path.description: Repo-relative POSIX path of this directory (e.g. `guild/web/pages`). Root dir uses `.`.

parent.description: Repo-relative POSIX path of the parent directory, or null for the repo root.

purpose.description: Optional one-line description of what the directory holds. Not overwritten on regeneration.

owners.description: Optional list of member IDs responsible for the directory. Not overwritten.

breadcrumb.description: Ordered ancestor ids from root → self.

children.description: Immediate subdirectory paths that also contain a `path.json`.

siblings.description: Peer directories sharing the same parent.

descendants.description: Count of all descendant FsPath instances under this dir.

file_count.description: Number of non-hidden files directly in this dir (excludes `path.json` itself).

route_bindings.description: URL-route Path ids (from `guild/web/components/udts/instances/paths/`) whose `page` target resolves inside this dir. Auto-computed — links fs-tree to site-map.

last_generated.description: UTC ISO timestamp of last regeneration.
